claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - he lives with someone else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or towards their accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, food, and other essentials.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

**How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep, and how often do you make these contributions (weekly, monthly, or in another arrangement)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that detail.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm a couple of final details:

**Are you responsible for Alex in the sense that you are their parent or legal guardian?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You are Alex's parent ✓
- Although Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets the requirement of paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

You meet all the eligibility criteria. Under the rules, you're considered responsible for a child if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards their upkeep - such as on food, clothes, or pocket money - and your weekly contribution qualifies you.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. If you have any questions about how to claim, please let me know!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant is Alex's parent and is contributing £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep. According to the eligibility rules, you'll usually be responsible for a child if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. The current Child Benefit rate for one child is £25.60 per week (for 2024/25), and the claimant is contributing £26.05 per week, which exceeds this amount. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant lives in the UK. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. Although Alex lives with someone else, the claimant contributes £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit amount, qualifying them as being responsible for the child under the rules. No one else is claiming for Alex, and the claimant lives in the UK.

The current Child Benefit rate for one child is £25.60 per week (for 2024/25), and the claimant is contributing £26.05 per week, which exceeds this amount. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant lives in the UK. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

No one else is claiming for Alex, and the claimant lives in the UK.
